taskDescription: Complete the crossword using terms from the submandibular region.

words.clue: Duct of the submandibular gland that opens beside the frenulum of the tongue
Muscle forming the floor of the mouth separating submandibular and sublingual spaces
Nerve that crosses Wharton's duct twice and is at risk during stone removal
Cranial nerve XII supplying all tongue muscles except palatoglossus
Life-threatening cellulitis of the floor of the mouth from dental infection
Condition caused by obstruction of a salivary duct by a calculus
Second largest salivary gland producing 70% of resting saliva
Muscle deep to the lingual nerve and CN XII that separates superficial from deep structures
Retention cyst of the sublingual gland appearing as a translucent floor-of-mouth swelling
Ganglion suspended from the lingual nerve that relays parasympathetic fibres to the submandibular gland

overallFeedback.feedback: Keep studying — review the material and try again.
Good effort — review the areas you missed.
Excellent work! You have a strong grasp of this topic.
